stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Sled-dog teams pulling a loaded sled can use up to 33% less energy when running in single-file formation than when running side by side.
  • The physics behind this savings has not been well understood.
  • Engineer Sofía Hernández used computational fluid dynamics to study airflow around a lead dog and its trailing teammates.
  • The simulations showed that the trailing dogs experience reduced air resistance because they run inside a low-pressure pocket created by the lead dog.
  • This effect reduces drag on the trailing dogs by about 45%.
stem: The student wants to present the study and its methodology. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Seeking to understand how trailing sled dogs save energy when running behind a lead dog, Sofía Hernández used computational fluid dynamics to study the airflow created by the lead dog.
A study showed that trailing sled dogs, which use up to 33% less energy when running behind a lead dog, also experience about 45% less drag.
Sofía Hernández studied the physics behind the fact that trailing dogs save energy by running in a low-pressure pocket.
Engineer Sofía Hernández found that trailing dogs are pulled along by the low-pressure pocket of a lead dog, reducing drag by about 45%.

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer because it presents both the study's question (why trailing dogs save energy) and its methodology (computational fluid dynamics simulations of airflow).

Choices B, C, and D report findings but don't present the study and methodology together.
